methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Transmutex, a Series A nuclear energy company founded by Servan-Schreiber, Carminati, and Revol, has raised $23M to date. The nuclear energy sector comprises 3 public peers (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private competitors including Fuse Energy, TerraPower, Kairos Power, X-Energy, and Oklo, providing broad sector context though limited direct comparables at Transmutex's stage and scale.

Valuation triangulation relies primarily on stage-progression methodology, as peer-median analysis is not applicable. The peer set in Nuclear Energy lacks sufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables meeting minimum thresholds (fewer than 3 datapoints in narrow filters), rendering median peer multiples unreliable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 2.0x multiplier to the $23M total raised as a valuation proxy, yielding an implied post-Series A value of $46M. This reflects typical step-up multiples observed in early-stage venture financing.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $39M to $53M, with a median of $46M. This narrow range reflects reliance on a single methodology and highlights the key uncertainty: absence of sufficiently dense peer comparables in nuclear energy at this stage. Any valuation conclusion carries material risk absent additional market data on recent Series A closings or later-stage funding rounds within the nuclear subsector. Investors should supplement this analysis with management guidance on use of proceeds and technical milestones that would justify higher-end multiples.
